fuzzy_prd:
We need a template engine that lets developers write HTML as a terse, indentation-based outline instead of hand-writing verbose angle-bracket tags. Each line in the source represents one element; nesting is expressed purely by indentation depth so closing tags are automatically inferred. The language should support compact shortcuts for attaching IDs and class names to elements, a flexible attribute model that sorts attributes alphabetically and handles boolean values cleanly (true renders as an empty attribute value, false omits the attribute entirely), inline and multi-line verbatim text blocks, dynamic expressions that HTML-escape by default with an opt-out for raw output, and string interpolation inside text and attribute values. The engine should support control-flow constructs like conditionals and loops, code comments that are stripped entirely, HTML comments, and doctype declarations that map short identifiers to the correct full declaration string. There should also be a pretty-printing mode that indents the output for readability. When the source is malformed, the engine must reject it before producing any output and report the exact line and column of the problem. Some constructs that the grammar accepts are still forbidden by the engine's rules — handle those consistently with how the parser reports its own errors. Attributes from a hash or key-value collection can be injected onto an element via a splat operator, and repeated class values from multiple sources should merge into a single space-separated list.

One small follow-up from the questions that came in: attribute ordering should be deterministic everywhere, so attributes are always emitted sorted alphabetically by name, regardless of the order they appear in the source. That applies across the whole final attribute set, including static attributes, anything added via shortcuts, and anything coming in through splatted attributes all merged together.

Also, if someone uses a shortcut like #id or .class without putting a tag name before it, we should treat that element as a 'div' by default.

And on errors, the formatting needs to stay exact. Syntax errors are reported as: 'error=syntax\nreason=<human-readable message>\nline=<1-based line number>\ncolumn=<1-based column number>\n'. Runtime errors for the forbidden-but-otherwise-parseable cases use: 'error=runtime\nreason=<message>\n' and do not include line/column.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with template/options/locals) -> stdout rendered HTML or normalized error

trigger_keywords: what is forbidden that the grammar accepts
what constructs are rejected as runtime errors
what triggers a runtime error rather than a syntax error
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Two constructs are forbidden by the engine even though they parse: (1) explicit '- end' block terminators (blocks should end by dedenting), giving reason 'Explicit end statements are forbidden'; and (2) specifying the id attribute more than once (whether via two shortcuts, or shortcut plus explicit attribute), giving reason 'Multiple id attributes specified'.

trigger_keywords: what does the pipe character do at the start of a line
how does a text block with pipe work
what is the difference between pipe and apostrophe text blocks
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A line beginning with '|' starts a verbatim text block; the following more-indented lines are emitted with relative indentation preserved, successive lines joined by newline. A line beginning with apostrophe (') does the same but appends a trailing space, so adjacent inline markup flows naturally.

trigger_keywords: how are nil and false attribute values handled
does a false attribute value render at all
what about nil class values in class merging
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A false or nil attribute value causes the attribute to be omitted entirely from the output. A nil class value and an empty-string class value are silently dropped during class merging.

Full Slim template engine surface: indentation and nesting, tag/class/id shorthand, attribute merging and boolean/nil handling, data attributes, text blocks, comments and preserved comments, doctype mappings, dynamic escaped/raw output, interpolation, loops/conditionals, whitespace controls, pretty-printing, locals, and syntax errors for invalid nesting.
Render the template with options and locals from stdin. Public_test_cases are authoritative for exact HTML, escaping, whitespace/newline behavior, doctypes, comment output, and error categories.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, and deterministic formatting. When prose and expected_output differ, expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: handle those consistently with how the parser reports its own errors
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Forbidden construct errors use the same neutral key=value output format as syntax errors but without line/column fields, using 'error=runtime' as the category. See feature17_forbidden_constructs.json.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific information about that detail. Please refer to the start.md specification and the public test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for authoritative requirements.

feature1_tag_nesting.json.description: Indentation-based nesting produces HTML with inferred closing tags. Elements are concatenated with no whitespace.

input.template:
html
  head
    title Simple Test Title
  body 
    p Hello World, meet Slim.

feature2_shortcuts.json.description: #id and .class shortcuts; bare shortcuts default to div; trailing colon for inline block.

feature4_boolean_attributes.json.description: true renders as empty value, false/nil omits the attribute.

input.template:
- cond=true
option selected=true Text
option selected=cond Text2

- cond=false
option selected=false Text
option selected=cond Text2

feature5_class_merging.json.description: Multiple class sources merge into one space-separated attribute; nil and empty dropped.

feature6_id_merging.json.description: Configurable join map can merge repeated id values.

feature7_data_attributes.json.description: Hash-valued attribute expands into dash-joined flattened attributes.

feature8_text_blocks.json.description: | begins verbatim text block with preserved relative indentation.

input.template:
p
  |
   Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

cases.expected_output: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

input.template:
p
 |
  This is line one.
   This is line two.
    This is line three.
     This is line four.
p This is a new paragraph.

cases.expected_output:
This is line one. This is line two. This is line three. This is line four.

This is a new paragraph.

feature9_dynamic_output.json.description: = escapes HTML, == does not. => adds trailing space.

feature10_interpolation.json.description: #{} HTML-escapes, #{{}} does not. \#{ escapes the construct.

feature11_control_flow.json.description: - lines carry control code: if/else/unless, case/when, loops.

input.template:
div
  - if false
      p The first paragraph
  - else
      p The second paragraph

feature12_comments.json.description: / is code comment (stripped). /! is HTML comment. /[ ] is conditional comment.

input.template:
p Hello
/ This is a comment
  Another comment
p World

p Hello
/! This is a comment

   Another comment
p World

feature13_doctype.json.description: doctype maps short identifier to full declaration string.

feature14_pretty.json.description: pretty=true puts block elements on their own lines with indentation.

feature15_splat.json.description: * injects key-value pairs as attributes; merges with other attributes sorted.

input.template: *{disabled: true, empty1: false, nonempty: '', empty2: nil} This is my title

feature16_syntax_errors.json.description: Malformed templates rejected with error=syntax, reason, line, column.

cases.expected_output:
error=syntax
reason=Unknown line indicator
line=6
column=2

feature17_forbidden_constructs.json.description: Explicit end statements and duplicate id are runtime errors.

input.template:
div
  - if true
      p A
  - end

cases.expected_output:
error=runtime
reason=Explicit end statements are forbidden
